req.title: System-Admin updates benefit
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a benefit through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for the requirement:
An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a benefit through the UI form.
Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def system_admin_user():
    """Return a fully-authenticated system-admin user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 1
    user.username = "admin_user"
    user.role = "system-admin"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def non_admin_user():
    """Return an authenticated user who is NOT a system-admin."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = 2
    user.username = "regular_user"
    user.role = "viewer"
    user.is_authenticated = True
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Return an unauthenticated (anonymous) user."""
    user = MagicMock()
    user.id = None
    user.username = None
    user.role = None
    user.is_authenticated = False
    return user


@pytest.fixture
def existing_benefit():
    """Return a benefit record that already exists in the system."""
    benefit = MagicMock()
    benefit.id = 42
    benefit.name = "Health Insurance"
    benefit.description = "Full health coverage for employees"
    benefit.value = 5000.00
    benefit.category = "Health"
    benefit.effective_date = "2024-01-01"
    benefit.is_active = True
    return benefit


@pytest.fixture
def valid_update_payload():
    """Return a valid form payload for updating a benefit."""
    return {
        "name": "Enhanced Health Insurance",
        "description": "Extended health coverage including dental",
        "value": 7500.00,
        "category": "Health",
        "effective_date": "2024-06-01",
        "is_active": True,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def audit_service():
    """Return a mock audit service."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def benefit_repository():
    """Return a mock benefit repository / data access layer."""
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def benefit_service(benefit_repository, audit_service):
    """
    Construct the system under test (BenefitService) with mocked
    collaborators injected.
    """
    from benefit_service import BenefitService  # adjust import to your project
    return BenefitService(
        repository=benefit_repository,
        audit_service=audit_service,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def ui_form_handler(benefit_service):
    """
    Construct the UI form handler (controller / view) under test.
    """
    from benefit_form_handler import BenefitFormHandler  # adjust to your project
    return BenefitFormHandler(benefit_service=benefit_service)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper – mandatory field names expected by the form
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

MANDATORY_FIELDS = ["name", "value", "category", "effective_date"]


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Authentication & authorisation guards
# ===========================================================================

class TestAccessControl:

    def test_system_admin_can_access_update_form(
        self, ui_form_handler, system_admin_user, existing_benefit
    ):
        """An authenticated system-admin must be able to open the update form."""
        response = ui_form_handler.get_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user, benefit_id=existing_benefit.id
        )
        assert response.status_code == 200

    def test_non_admin_cannot_access_update_form(
        self, ui_form_handler, non_admin_user, existing_benefit
    ):
        """A non-admin authenticated user must be denied access (403)."""
        response = ui_form_handler.get_update_form(
            user=non_admin_user, benefit_id=existing_benefit.id
        )
        assert response.status_code == 403

    def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_access_update_form(
        self, ui_form_handler, unauthenticated_user, existing_benefit
    ):
        """An unauthenticated user must be redirected / denied (401 or 302)."""
        response = ui_form_handler.get_update_form(
            user=unauthenticated_user, benefit_id=existing_benefit.id
        )
        assert response.status_code in (401, 302)

    def test_non_admin_cannot_submit_update(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        non_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """A non-admin must not be able to POST an update, even with valid data."""
        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=non_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=valid_update_payload,
        )
        assert response.status_code == 403

    def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_submit_update(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        unauthenticated_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """An unauthenticated user must not be able to POST an update."""
        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=unauthenticated_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=valid_update_payload,
        )
        assert response.status_code in (401, 302)


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Successful update – happy path
# ===========================================================================

class TestSuccessfulUpdate:

    def test_valid_submission_returns_success_response(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
    ):
        """A valid form submission by a system-admin must succeed (200 / 302)."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit
        benefit_repository.update.return_value = True

        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=valid_update_payload,
        )
        assert response.status_code in (200, 302)

    def test_benefit_fields_are_persisted_correctly(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
    ):
        """All submitted field values must be passed to the repository for persistence."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit
        benefit_repository.update.return_value = True

        ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        benefit_repository.update.assert_called_once()
        _, kwargs = benefit_repository.update.call_args
        saved = kwargs.get("benefit") or benefit_repository.update.call_args[0][0]

        assert saved.name == valid_update_payload["name"]
        assert saved.description == valid_update_payload["description"]
        assert saved.value == valid_update_payload["value"]
        assert saved.category == valid_update_payload["category"]
        assert saved.effective_date == valid_update_payload["effective_date"]

    def test_success_message_shown_in_response(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
    ):
        """The response must contain a user-facing success message."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit
        benefit_repository.update.return_value = True

        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=valid_update_payload,
        )
        assert hasattr(response, "message") or response.status_code in (200, 302)
        if hasattr(response, "message"):
            assert "success" in response.message.lower()

    def test_update_form_pre_populated_with_existing_values(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        benefit_repository,
    ):
        """The GET form must be pre-populated with the benefit's current values."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit

        response = ui_form_handler.get_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user, benefit_id=existing_benefit.id
        )

        assert response.status_code == 200
        form_data = response.context.get("form") or response.form_data
        assert form_data["name"] == existing_benefit.name
        assert form_data["value"] == existing_benefit.value
        assert form_data["category"] == existing_benefit.category

    def test_nonexistent_benefit_returns_404(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        benefit_repository,
        valid_update_payload,
    ):
        """Attempting to update a benefit that doesn't exist must return 404."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = None

        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=9999,
            form_data=valid_update_payload,
        )
        assert response.status_code == 404


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Mandatory field validation
# ===========================================================================

class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_fails_validation(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
        missing_field,
    ):
        """
        Submitting a form with any single mandatory field absent must
        result in a validation error (400) and must NOT persist data.
        """
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit

        payload = {**valid_update_payload}
        del payload[missing_field]

        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code == 400
        benefit_repository.update.assert_not_called()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("empty_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_empty_mandatory_field_fails_validation(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
        empty_field,
    ):
        """
        Submitting a form with any mandatory field set to an empty string
        must result in a validation error (400) and must NOT persist data.
        """
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit

        payload = {**valid_update_payload, empty_field: ""}

        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code == 400
        benefit_repository.update.assert_not_called()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("null_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_null_mandatory_field_fails_validation(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
        null_field,
    ):
        """
        Submitting a form with any mandatory field set to None must result
        in a validation error (400) and must NOT persist data.
        """
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit

        payload = {**valid_update_payload, null_field: None}

        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code == 400
        benefit_repository.update.assert_not_called()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", MANDATORY_FIELDS)
    def test_validation_error_message_references_missing_field(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
        missing_field,
    ):
        """
        The validation error response must include an error message that
        references the missing mandatory field by name.
        """
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit

        payload = {**valid_update_payload}
        del payload[missing_field]

        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code == 400
        errors = response.errors if hasattr(response, "errors") else {}
        assert missing_field in errors or missing_field in str(response.content)

    def test_negative_value_fails_validation(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
    ):
        """Benefit value must be a positive number; negative values are invalid."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit

        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "value": -100.00}

        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code == 400
        benefit_repository.update.assert_not_called()

    def test_invalid_date_format_fails_validation(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
    ):
        """An effective_date in an invalid format must fail validation."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit

        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "effective_date": "not-a-date"}

        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code == 400
        benefit_repository.update.assert_not_called()

    def test_all_mandatory_fields_present_passes_validation(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
    ):
        """When all mandatory fields are supplied with valid data, validation passes."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit
        benefit_repository.update.return_value = True

        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code in (200, 302)
        benefit_repository.update.assert_called_once()


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Audit record
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditRecord:

    def test_audit_record_written_on_successful_update(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """An audit record must be created when a benefit is successfully updated."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit
        benefit_repository.update.return_value = True

        ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        audit_service.write.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_actor_identity(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record must capture which user performed the update."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit
        benefit_repository.update.return_value = True

        ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        audit_service.write.assert_called_once()
        audit_entry = audit_service.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_entry.user_id == system_admin_user.id
        assert audit_entry.username == system_admin_user.username

    def test_audit_record_contains_benefit_id(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record must reference the ID of the benefit that was updated."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit
        benefit_repository.update.return_value = True

        ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        audit_entry = audit_service.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_entry.entity_id == existing_benefit.id

    def test_audit_record_contains_action_type(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record must indicate the action type is UPDATE."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit
        benefit_repository.update.return_value = True

        ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        audit_entry = audit_service.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_entry.action.upper() == "UPDATE"

    def test_audit_record_contains_timestamp(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record must contain a non-null timestamp."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit
        benefit_repository.update.return_value = True

        before = datetime.utcnow()
        ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=valid_update_payload,
        )
        after = datetime.utcnow()

        audit_entry = audit_service.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_entry.timestamp is not None
        assert before <= audit_entry.timestamp <= after

    def test_audit_record_captures_changed_fields(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """The audit record should capture the before-and-after field values."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit
        benefit_repository.update.return_value = True

        ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        audit_entry = audit_service.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert hasattr(audit_entry, "changes") and audit_entry.changes is not None
        assert "name" in audit_entry.changes
        assert audit_entry.changes["name"]["before"] == existing_benefit.name
        assert audit_entry.changes["name"]["after"] == valid_update_payload["name"]

    def test_audit_record_not_written_on_validation_failure(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """When validation fails the audit service must NOT be called."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit

        payload = {**valid_update_payload}
        del payload["name"]

        ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=payload,
        )

        audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_record_not_written_on_persistence_failure(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """When the repository raises an error the audit service must NOT be called."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit
        benefit_repository.update.side_effect = Exception("DB connection error")

        with pytest.raises(Exception):
            ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
                user=system_admin_user,
                benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
                form_data=valid_update_payload,
            )

        audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_record_not_written_when_unauthorised(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        non_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """An unauthorised submission attempt must not produce an audit record."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit

        ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=non_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=valid_update_payload,
        )

        audit_service.write.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 5. Idempotency / edge cases
# ===========================================================================

class TestEdgeCases:

    def test_update_with_unchanged_values_still_writes_audit(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        benefit_repository,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """
        Submitting the form with identical values to what already exists
        should still complete successfully and write an audit record
        (the user explicitly chose to confirm the data).
        """
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit
        benefit_repository.update.return_value = True

        unchanged_payload = {
            "name": existing_benefit.name,
            "description": existing_benefit.description,
            "value": existing_benefit.value,
            "category": existing_benefit.category,
            "effective_date": existing_benefit.effective_date,
            "is_active": existing_benefit.is_active,
        }

        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=unchanged_payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code in (200, 302)
        audit_service.write.assert_called_once()

    def test_whitespace_only_name_fails_validation(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """A name consisting solely of whitespace must be treated as empty."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit

        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "name": "   "}

        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code == 400
        benefit_repository.update.assert_not_called()
        audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_extremely_long_name_fails_validation(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
    ):
        """A name exceeding the maximum allowed length must fail validation."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit

        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "name": "A" * 1001}

        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code == 400
        benefit_repository.update.assert_not_called()

    def test_zero_value_fails_validation(
        self,
        ui_form_handler,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
    ):
        """Benefit value of zero must be rejected as it is not meaningful."""
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit

        payload = {**valid_update_payload, "value": 0}

        response = ui_form_handler.submit_update_form(
            user=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            form_data=payload,
        )

        assert response.status_code == 400
        benefit_repository.update.assert_not_called()

    def test_concurrent_updates_each_produce_separate_audit_records(
        self,
        benefit_service,
        system_admin_user,
        existing_benefit,
        valid_update_payload,
        benefit_repository,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """
        Two sequential updates must each produce their own audit record
        (basic concurrency / idempotency check at the service level).
        """
        benefit_repository.get_by_id.return_value = existing_benefit
        benefit_repository.update.return_value = True

        benefit_service.update_benefit(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            data=valid_update_payload,
        )
        benefit_service.update_benefit(
            actor=system_admin_user,
            benefit_id=existing_benefit.id,
            data={**valid_update_payload, "value": 9000.00},
        )

        assert audit_service.write.call_count == 2
```

---

### Notes for implementers

| Area | Decision rationale |
|---|---|
| **Fixture-based mocking** | All external collaborators (repository, audit service) are injected as `MagicMock` objects, keeping tests fast and deterministic with no I/O. |
| **`MANDATORY_FIELDS` parametrisation** | Iterating over the list with `@pytest.mark.parametrize` ensures every mandatory field is exercised independently without duplicating test bodies. |
| **Audit negative cases** | Tests explicitly assert `audit_service.write.assert_not_called()` for validation failures, persistence errors, and unauthorised requests to prevent false-positive audit trails. |
| **Import paths** | Replace `from benefit_service import BenefitService` and `from benefit_form_handler import BenefitFormHandler` with the real module paths in your project. |
| **`response.context` / `response.form_data`** | Adjust attribute access to match whatever your framework (Django, Flask, FastAPI, etc.) returns from the handler under test. |
